summary: The CMOVNZ selectors immediately before the session/open and session/close submit calls are small-string pointer selectors. The builders first pass the inline stack buffer address, then test bit 7 of the local string control/tag byte; when that high bit is set, CMOVNZ replaces the inline address with the heap pointer stored in the local string object.

predicate_pattern.interpretation: bit7(control byte) selects heap-backed string storage; clear means inline small-string buffer address remains live
predicate_pattern.why_it_matters: Submitter arguments are normalized char pointers, not two different endpoint forms. This explains why RDX/R8 were built with LEA defaults followed by conditional memory loads.

body_or_payload_argument.role: body/query string buffer prepared by helper 0x140032150
body/query string buffer prepared by helper 0x140032150

server_implications: OpenNXT endpoint parity should treat the submitted URL/body as ordinary normalized string pointers after local small-string selection, not as separate inline-vs-heap request variants.
The client-side branch condition is string-storage representation, not auth/session success. Server tests should assert endpoint content rather than CMOV branch shape.
The same small-string selector pattern can be searched elsewhere around OTL/directlogin submitters to normalize argument recovery.
next: Add a reusable small-string selector recognizer if more submitter callsites need the same bit7-control-byte normalization.
Use the normalized session/open and session/close endpoint contracts to seed OpenNXT 947 request logging/stub coverage.
